subject: Ecology
title: Air Quality and Ecosystems
description: A 60-second lesson on Air Quality and Ecosystems.

hook.text: Urban air pollution doesn't just harm lungs. It changes how ecosystems function around cities.

buildup.text: Nitrogen oxides from traffic fertilize roadsides, boosting weedy species over native plants.

discovery.text: Ground-level ozone damages leaf tissue, reducing photosynthesis in urban and suburban trees.

twist.text: Lichens vanish from polluted city centers—ecologists use them as air quality indicators.

climax.text: Cleaner air policies in many cities have measurably increased urban tree health and lichen return.

punchline.text: Clean the air and the ecosystems follow.

quiz.question: Why are lichens used as air quality indicators?
options: They only grow on clean metal
